description: Dr. David Dao is forcibly removed from United Express Flight 3411 by aviation security officers after refusing to give up his seat for airline crew. | Videos of the violent removal go viral on social media, sparking global outrage and widespread condemnation of United Airlines' handling of the situation.
United Airlines CEO Oscar Munoz issues an initial statement apologizing for having to 're-accommodate' customers, which is widely criticized as tone-deaf.
United Airlines stock price drops significantly following the public relations fallout, wiping out nearly $1 billion in market value.
CEO Oscar Munoz issues a second, more contrite apology, taking full responsibility and promising a thorough review of airline policies.
United Airlines reaches an undisclosed settlement with Dr. David Dao to resolve claims related to the incident.
United Airlines announces a new policy allowing passengers to receive up to $10,000 in compensation for voluntarily giving up their seats.
